nameMeanings.name: Konrad
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Franz, Horst, Gerhard, Armin, Arno, Bernhard, Ernst, Florian.

German, Polish, Slovenian, and Croatian; Czech, Slovak, and Hungarian (Konrád): from the personal name Konrad, composed of the ancient Germanic elements kuoni ‘daring, experienced’ + rād, rāt ‘counsel’. This fell together at an early date with another ancient Germanic name, of which the first element was chunni or kuni ‘race, people’. Konrad was extremely popular as a personal name in central Europe during the Middle Ages, being a hereditary name in several princely families as well as enjoying widespread popularity among the people at large. It was the name of several Christian saints, including a 10th-century bishop of Constance. It was also adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews in German-speaking provinces of Central and Eastern Europe as a Germanized form of Kohn or Cohn . Compare Conrad .

variants.name: Conrado
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Bayardo, Carlos, Eduardo, Adriano, Alphonso, Cesar, Concepcion, Fabiola, Francisco, Jaime, Jeronimo, Jorge.
Spanish: from the personal name Conrado, of ancient Germanic origin(see Konrad ).

variants.name: Coonradt
meanings: Americanized form of Dutch Coenraet or Koenraadt or German Kühnrat (see Konrad ).

variants.name: Conrad
meanings: German: variant of   Konrad   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. It was also adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews.
French (mainly Alsace and Lorraine): from the personal name Conrad, of ancient Germanic origin, or a Frenchified form of the German cognate Konrad .
English (Middlesex): from an Anglicized form of the German personal name   Konrad  .

variants.name: Coonrod
meanings: Americanized form of Dutch Coenraet or Koenraadt or German Kühnrat (see Konrad ).

variants.name: Corrado
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Luigi, Antonio, Domenic, Enrico, Pasquale, Sal, Salvatore, Alessandro, Attilio, Carlo, Cosmo.
Italian: from the personal name Corrado, from ancient Germanic Chuonrad (see Konrad ).

variants.name: Conradi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dietrich, Ewald, Hannelore.
German, Danish, Norwegian, and Swedish: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name Konrad or Conrad , using the genitive of the Latinized form Conradus. Compare Conrady .
Italian: variant of   Corradi  .

variants.name: Conrady
meanings: German: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name   Konrad   or   Conrad  , a variant of   Conradi  .

variants.name: Kohn
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Meyer, Chaim, Aron, Moshe, Chaya, Emanuel, Mendel, Amron, Gitty, Leib, Mayer.  German Kurt, Fritz, Erwin, Gunther, Manfred, Gerhard, Bernhard, Erna, Hans, Armin, Egon.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Cohen  .
North German (also Köhn): from the personal name Kohn or Köhn, old Low German short forms of Konrad .

variants.name: Conk
meanings: Probably an Americanized form of German Konk or some other similar (like-sounding) German surname derived from an old short form of the personal name Konrad .

variants.name: Conradt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Ernst, Hans, Heinz, Horst.
German: variant of   Konrad  .
